_stage: CPAL Stage 1 — Wrong-Model Identification + LP Level Classification
_grounded_in: LP rubric framing: Jin et al. 2019/2025; plateau rule: Chi et al. 1989/1994, Renkl 2002

_input_recap.student_message: My Java program keeps crashing with NullPointerException. I created a Room object but calling describe() on it blows up. I've tried adding initializers but nothing works. Can you help me figure out what I'm missing?
diagnostic_phrases_extracted: calling describe() on it blows up
I've tried adding initializers but nothing works

wrong_model_identification.wrong_model_description: Declaring a variable creates the object. String s; creates a String object named s. The name and the thing are the same act.
wrong_model_identification.wrong_model_origin: Python/scripting: name = thing is one act. Natural language: naming implies existence.
wrong_model_identification.matched_signal: I created s right there at the top
wrong_model_identification.match_evidence: Student says 'I created a Room object' — but the code shown only has 'Room currentRoom;' (declaration, no instantiation). The verb 'created' applied to a mere declaration is the diagnostic NP-A signal: he believes declaring is the same act as creating.

alternatives_considered.wrong_belief_short: null means empty or zero; calling a method on null should return empty/default, not crash
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Arjun does not express that null should 'just return nothing'; his confusion is upstream — he doesn't believe currentRoom is null in the first place.

alternatives_considered.wrong_belief_short: The variable holds the object itself, not a pointer to it; no concept of indirection
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: NP-C and NP-A overlap, but Arjun's specific phrasing ('I created … but it blows up when I call …') points at a model of declaration as creation, not at the absence-of-indirection model. NP-A is the more specific match.

semantic_wm_top3.signal: I created s right there at the top

lp_signals_detected.signal: Reports NPE symptom but cannot say why
lp_signals_detected.evidence: 'keeps crashing with NullPointerException' + 'figure out what I'm missing'
lp_signals_detected.rubric_match: L1: 'Knows NPE happened, cannot say why'

lp_signals_detected.signal: Cannot answer what currentRoom holds after `Room currentRoom;`
lp_signals_detected.evidence: Believes he 'created' it by declaring it (NP-A wrong model)
lp_signals_detected.rubric_match: L1: "Cannot answer 'what does s hold after String s;'"

lp_signals_detected.signal: Does not name 'null' as the cause
lp_signals_detected.evidence: Student says 'tried initializers' — vague, not 'I never assigned with new'
lp_signals_detected.rubric_match: Below L2 floor: L2 requires 'Knows null means no object assigned'

rubric_definitions.L1_current: Knows NPE happened, cannot say why. Cannot answer 'what does s hold after String s;'
rubric_definitions.L2_target: Knows null means 'no object assigned'. Knows the fix is to initialise with new. Cannot trace the dereference mechanism.
rubric_definitions.L3_future: Can trace: declaration creates a reference slot holding null. new allocates a heap object and returns its address. Method call dereferences the reference — follows the address to the heap object. null dereference fails because there is no address to follow → NPE. Can predict which line throws NPE.
rubric_definitions.L4_future: Applies to all reference types. Understands why null checks are necessary before method calls. Can write null-safe code. Understands Optional as a design pattern.

plateau_gate.rule: Triggers iff current_lp_level == L2 AND lp_streak >= 2 on this concept
plateau_gate.current_state: L1, streak=1 → not eligible for plateau (which only applies at L2)

expert_benchmark_key_ideas: A reference variable in Java is a slot that can hold either null or a heap address — it is not the object itself.
`Room currentRoom;` allocates the slot. The slot starts at null.
`new Room()` allocates a Room on the heap and returns its address. Assignment puts that address in the slot.
Calling `currentRoom.describe()` follows the address in the slot. If the slot is null, there is no address to follow — NullPointerException.

downstream_effects.stage_2_intervention_filter: Valid intervention set restricted to L1→L2 transitions (visual_analogy, scaffolded_explanation, socratic_recognition). Direct mechanism trace (L3 territory) excluded — too steep.

stage_3_prompt_sections.LP-1_current_understanding: Populated from L1 rubric — student treats declaration as creation
stage_3_prompt_sections.LP-2_wrong_model_context: NP-A wrong belief + Python/natural-language origin — LLM is told NOT to assume student knows null is a thing yet
stage_3_prompt_sections.LP-3_target_understanding: Populated from L2 rubric — by end of session, student should be able to say 'currentRoom is null because I never assigned a Room to it with new'

downstream_effects.stage_4_delta_lp_baseline: L1 (current). After student's reply, recompute LP and store delta_lp = new_level - L1.
downstream_effects.stage_5_persist_to_skg: Write all fields above to Student Knowledge Graph under arjun.cognitive_graph.concept_nodes.null_pointer (extends existing entry).
